data.title: Fusen Pharmaceutical Wins China Approval for Glucosamine Osteoarthritis Capsule
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Fusen Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. ( (HK:1652) ) is now available.

Fusen Pharmaceutical has secured Chinese regulatory approval for its Glucosamine Sulfate Capsule, marketed under the Shuhuitong brand, for treating primary and secondary osteoarthritis. The product, based on natural amino monosaccharides that support cartilage matrix synthesis, is positioned to relieve joint pain, improve function, and slow disease progression in patients needing both short-term relief and long-term maintenance.

Classified as a Class B drug in the national medical insurance catalogue, Glucosamine Sulfate Capsule already represents a sizeable and fast-growing market segment, with category sales exceeding RMB850 million in 2024 and strong double-digit growth into 2025. The launch strengthens Fusen Pharmaceutical’s product pipeline in musculoskeletal care, enhances its presence in the osteoarthritis field, and taps into rising demand driven by China’s aging population and the broader Healthy China policy agenda.

More about Fusen Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd.

Fusen Pharmaceutical Company Limited is a Hong Kong–listed drug maker incorporated in the Cayman Islands, operating through subsidiaries such as Henan Fusen Pharmaceutical in mainland China. The group focuses on developing and marketing prescription medicines in therapeutic areas aligned with long-term demographic trends, targeting the growing demand for chronic disease treatments in the Chinese healthcare market.

Average Trading Volume: 614,368

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$882.3M

XD, Inc. ( (HK:2400) ) has issued an update.

XD Inc. has granted 59,923 share options under its 2021 share option plan to eligible directors and full-time employees, with each option allowing subscription for one ordinary share at an exercise price of HK$64.20. All options vest immediately on the grant date and carry a ten-year validity, aligning employee incentives with the company’s long-term growth.

Of the total grant, 44,922 options were awarded to chairman, chief executive officer and executive director Mr. Huang Yimeng, a substantial shareholder, following approval by the independent non-executive directors. The board and its remuneration and appraisal committee emphasize that the grants form part of market-aligned compensation intended to recognize key leadership responsibilities and reinforce continued contributions to the group’s development, without breaching individual or regulatory limits.

data.title: CPI Property Group Launches €3.00-Per-Share Takeover Offer to Delist Next RE SIIQ
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from NEXT RE SIIQ S.p.A. ( (IT:NR) ) is now available.

CPI Property Group has published the offer document for its voluntary public tender offer to acquire up to 4,413,586 ordinary shares of Next RE SIIQ at €3.00 per share in cash, with the goal of amassing more than 90% of the company and delisting it from Euronext Milan. The offer period will run from 20 April to 15 May 2026, with possible reopening and extensions, and if fully accepted would imply a maximum cash outlay of about €13.24 million, tightening CPI’s grip on the Italian vehicle and eliminating free float for minority shareholders.

The tender process will be conducted exclusively in Italy, with acceptances collected via BNP Paribas’ Italian branch and Equita SIM, and settlement scheduled for 22 May 2026 unless the timetable shifts. The move underscores CPI Property Group’s strategy to fully integrate Next RE, streamline governance and operations outside the public market, and could mark another step in the broader consolidation trend in European listed real estate as sponsors seek greater control over their platforms.

More about NEXT RE SIIQ S.p.A.

Next RE SIIQ S.p.A. is an Italian real estate investment company whose ordinary shares are listed on Euronext Milan. It operates within the property sector, with its shareholder base now the target of a full takeover by CPI Property Group S.A., a Luxembourg-based real estate group seeking to consolidate control and remove the stock from public trading.

Average Trading Volume: 826

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: €32.49M

Gaming Realms ( (GB:GMR) ) just unveiled an update.

Gaming Realms PLC has continued its share buyback programme, repurchasing 7,693 ordinary shares at 32.30 pence each on 16 April 2026 via broker Peel Hunt and placing them into treasury. Following this transaction, the company now holds 19,160,026 shares in treasury, with 277,105,988 ordinary shares remaining in issue, which constitutes the total voting rights for shareholders’ disclosure calculations.

The move marginally reduces the free float and may support earnings per share over time, signalling ongoing capital management and confidence in the company’s valuation. The updated voting rights figure provides clarity for investors assessing their notifiable interests under UK disclosure and transparency rules, ensuring compliance while the buyback programme progresses.

data.title: Beijing Energy Explains Lapses in Disclosing Connected Power Trades
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Beijing Energy International Holding ( (HK:0686) ) has issued an update.

Beijing Energy International issued a supplemental announcement explaining past non-compliance with Hong Kong connected transaction rules, after failing to make timely disclosures on certain electricity trading agreements with connected persons. The company said the omission arose as China’s unified national power market expanded and transactions increasingly took place via standardized power trading platforms, where contracts are formed through electronic confirmations and settlements are handled by grid companies.

An internal review found that the group’s connected-transaction controls were designed mainly for traditional written contracts and direct fund flows, and did not adequately capture indirect connected transactions conducted through third-party trading platforms. The company also cited insufficient familiarity among some frontline subsidiary staff with Chapter 14A of the Listing Rules and substance-over-form requirements, which delayed escalation and disclosure of the relevant electricity transactions.

More about Beijing Energy International Holding

Beijing Energy International Holding Co., Ltd. is a Hong Kong-listed power producer focused on photovoltaic and wind power generation. The company participates in China’s evolving national power market, engaging in interprovincial and intraprovincial power trading via standardized, regulated trading platforms rather than direct bilateral contracts with counterparties.

Average Trading Volume: 740,933

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$2.33B

The disclosure reinforces Kenmare’s regulatory compliance and transparency for investors across its dual listings in London and Dublin. By making the detailed annual report widely available, the titanium minerals producer provides stakeholders with enhanced visibility into its operations at the Moma mine and its position in the global titanium feedstocks market.

data.title: Graphex Group to Terminate U.S. ADS Programme and Refocus on Hong Kong Listing
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Graphex Group ADR ( (GRFXY) ) just unveiled an update.

Graphex Group Limited has moved to voluntarily terminate its sponsored American Depositary Shares programme in the United States, where its ADSs currently trade on the OTC Expert Market under the symbol GRFXY. The decision follows years of declining U.S. listing status, including the May 2025 delisting of its ADSs from NYSE American and their migration to over-the-counter trading.

On April 10, 2026, the company instructed The Bank of New York Mellon to end the deposit agreement, and the depositary notified ADS holders on April 16, 2026 that the programme will terminate on July 14, 2026. Investors have until at least July 17, 2026 to surrender ADSs for the underlying Hong Kong–listed ordinary shares, after which the depositary may sell remaining shares and remit net proceeds, while Graphex will focus its listing and fundraising activities on its primary Hong Kong market.

Spark’s Take on GRFXY Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GRFXY is a Neutral.

Graphex Group ADR’s overall stock score is primarily impacted by its weak financial performance and poor valuation metrics. The technical analysis further indicates bearish momentum, with the stock trading below key moving averages and nearing oversold conditions. The lack of profitability and absence of a dividend yield make the stock less attractive to investors.

To see Spark’s full report on GRFXY stock, click here.

More about Graphex Group ADR

Graphex Group Limited is a Hong Kong–listed company (HKSE: 6128) whose ordinary shares trade on the Main Board of The Stock Exchange of Hong Kong. The company previously accessed U.S. capital markets through a sponsored American Depositary Share programme, with ADSs representing its Hong Kong–listed ordinary shares and having been traded on venues including NYSE American and, more recently, the OTC Expert Market.

data.title: Shenzhen Han’s CNC Sets 2025 AGM to Vote on Capital, Payout and Financing Plans
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shenzhen Han’s CNC Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3200) ) has issued an announcement.

Shenzhen Han’s CNC Technology has convened its 2025 annual general meeting for 8 May 2026 in Shenzhen, where H-shareholders recorded by early May will be eligible to attend or vote by proxy. The agenda covers approval of the 2025 board work report, annual report, final financial accounts, profit distribution plan, a 2026 bank financing plan, re-appointment of auditors and a remuneration system for directors and senior management.

Shareholders will also vote on reports related to the deposit, management and use of proceeds from the company’s 2025 A-share offering, including the use of idle proceeds and self-owned funds for cash management. A special resolution seeks approval to change the company’s registered capital and amend its articles of association, signaling potential adjustments to its capital structure and governance that could influence future financing flexibility and shareholder returns.

More about Shenzhen Han’s CNC Technology Co., Ltd. Class H

Shenzhen Han’s CNC Technology Co., Ltd. is a mainland China-based manufacturer in the industrial equipment sector, operating as part of the Han’s Laser group. The company focuses on CNC and intelligent manufacturing solutions, serving industrial clients in China and overseas through its H-share listing in Hong Kong, which provides access to international capital markets.
